notes: [values] damage-pipeline.dispatch.teamplay_shaping: In-code fallbacks still differ from stock Base defaults: CvarTeamplayMode fallback 2 (DamageSystem.cs) vs xonotic-server.cfg:279 teamplay_mode 4; CvarFriendlyFireVirtualForce fallback 0 vs xonotic-server.cfg:285 g_friendlyfire_virtual_force 1. Cvars read live, so correct when the server cfg is loaded; a headless run with no cfg behaves wrong. Unchanged from baseline. ; complainTeamDamage is USED for the mode-4 mirror multiplier (DamageSystem.Apply:202-203), but the team-kill complaint SOUND timer (teamkill_soundtime/teamkill_complain) is still NOT wired (Apply:268 `_ = complainTeamDamage` disc

[logic] damage-pipeline.fire.burning: Fire TRANSFER is still entirely missing: QC Fire_ApplyDamage:1094-1104 re-ignites every overlapping non-frozen non-independent g_damagedbycontents entity at firetransfer_damage 0.8 over firetransfer_time 0.9 — left as a documented host-loop TODO in FireApplyDamage. Burning never chains between bunched-up players. Unchanged from baseline. ; Napalm (NadeNapalmBoom.cs:190) still ignites via the raw StatusEffectsCatalog.Apply(strength=dps) path rather than FireAddDamage: the per-tick DPS is correct (Strength fallback), but it does NOT use the overlap LEMMA merge (stacked napalm fire overwrites ins

[logic/values] damage-pipeline.heal.dispatcher: QC Heal(targ, inflictor, amount, limit) (server/damage.qc:948-958) — the symmetric counterpart to Damage(): game_stopped / IS_DEAD / frozen rejection, the .event_heal entity callback route, and the per-target limit cap — still has no port analogue. Healing items / regen / medkits add health ad-hoc per entity with no shared gate, so a frozen/dead/game-stopped heal is not centrally blocked and there is no event_heal hook seam. Unchanged from baseline.

[logic/values] net-entity-state.entcs.privacy_mask: No ent_cs enemy-privacy mask (radar_showenemies/SAME_TEAM): every player's origin/health/armor is sent to everyone, PVS-gated only — Base strips private fields for non-teammates.

[logic/values/timing] net-entity-state.wepent.switch_alpha_misc: Remote weapon switch animation coarse: only the resolved active weapon is networked, not wepent m_switchweapon/m_switchingweapon — a remote player's weapon raise/lower transition is not reproduced. ; wepent m_alpha (weapon fade), m_gunalign, porto_v_angle_held (Porto held-angle viewmodel), tuba_instrument, viewmodel skin are not networked.

[values] net-entity-state.replicate.client_cvars: Three replicated cvars are stored server-side but have no server-side consumer yet: cl_autoscreenshot, cl_clippedspectating, g_xonoticversion are now in SentCvarAllowlist (Commands.cs:194-197) so they are accepted+stored per-client, but nothing reads them on the server (autoscreenshot is still driven by Intermission's broadcast stuffcmd dance, not the per-client replicated flag; clippedspectating + g_xonoticversion are client-only / unenforced). Narrower than the baseline (they are no longer dropped at the gate), but still not honored per-player.

[logic] resources.api.give: GiveResource is faithful (GiveResource hook + per-resource rot-pause push). GiveResourceWithLimit STILL does NOT fire the QC GiveResourceWithLimit mutator hook (sv_resources.qc:165) — Wave-2 added only an explanatory comment; ResourceHooks has no GiveResourceWithLimit chain. The port trims the amount and delegates to GiveResource (which fires the GiveResource hook). No shipped mutator subscribes to the WithLimit chain, so latent; flagged for completeness.

[logic] resources.api.take: Port TakeResource STILL omits the QC TakeResource mutator hook (sv_resources.qc:191 MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(TakeResource)) — Wave-2 added only a comment; ResourceHooks has no TakeResource chain. A mutator can therefore not forbid/rewrite a resource drain. No shipped mutator subscribes, so latent. ; TakeResourceWithLimit IS ported (Wave-2) and the SVQC clamp formula is bit-faithful (current-amount < -limit -> amount = current-limit, i.e. the total can't go below -limit). It IS live: ItemPickupRules.GiveAmmoTo:246 calls it for the negative-amount (drain) item branch, mirroring Base items.qc:507 (Item_G

[logic] resources.regen.loop: PlayerRegen hook STILL carries only Player (MutatorHooks.cs:107-112 PlayerRegenArgs unchanged) — all 10 in/out tuning slots remain missing. Consequences: (a) max_mod scaling of the health regen/rot STABLE point (client.qc:1725-1726) is never applied; (b) regen_mod/rot_mod speed scaling (multiply the frametime) and limit_mod cap scaling are inert. limit_mod is not even a parameter of the port's RotRegen (Base RotRegen takes limit_mod and clamps to GetResourceLimit*limit_mod; the port clamps to raw GetResourceLimit). All four mods are 1 at stock, so default play is unaffected; the gap bites muta

[logic] resources.regen.pause_timers: On spawn during the warmup countdown, Base extends each primed pause timer by (game_starttime - time) so timers don't elapse while frozen pre-match (client.qc:665-669). The port primes the four spawn pause timers (SpawnSystem.cs:611-614, defaults 0/5/5/10 verified) but STILL does NOT add the countdown offset — the SpawnSystem comment explicitly defers it ('re-apply the (game_starttime - time) shield/pause extension once the spawn path runs with a match-time handle'). Minor: only observable if a player can regen/rot during the pre-match countdown. Unchanged by Wave-2.

[logic/timing] scoring.lifecycle.game_stopped: STILL OPEN (no change from baseline): the central game_stopped clamp is inert. GameScores.AddToPlayer (GameScores.cs:421) and AddToTeam (GameScores.cs:494) check the static GameScores.GameStopped, but no server/live code ever sets it true — grep `GameScores.GameStopped =` over src finds ZERO assignments (only test setup). GameWorld uses its own GameStopped property + per-call-site guards instead. Several readers exist (Domination, TargetUtilities, MonsterAI, WeaponAccuracyEvents) but they read a flag that is never raised. Most scoring goes through gametype handlers that gate on MatchEnded/inte

[logic/timing] sv-anticheat.detect.div0_evade: STILL OPEN: AntiCheat.PreThink (zeroes evade_offset) has no live caller. OnClientMove (GameWorld.cs:1290-1294) fires MutatorHooks.PlayerPreThink but never AntiCheat.PreThink(p), so the div0_evade reseed branch never re-arms per frame; the detector accumulates against a single stale phase sample and the div0_evade verdict diverges from Base for identical player behavior.

[logic/values/timing] sv-anticheat.frame.fixangle_window: STILL OPEN: Teleporter fixangle suppression not wired. Teleporters.cs:309 sets player.FixAngle but Teleporters.cs has zero AntiCheat references, so after a teleport snap-aim/strafebot_new is not suppressed and a legit teleport view-snap can inflate the strafebot_new / idle_snapaim signal (false positive). ; STILL OPEN: EndFrame's FOREACH_CLIENT(.fixangle) propagation is dead. GameWorld.cs:1540 always calls EndFrame(Simulation.FrameTime, Time) with no fixAngleClients, so any non-spawn forced view change never opens the suppression window. ; STILL OPEN: PingProvider hardcoded to 0 (GameWorld.cs:

[logic/values] sv-anticheat.report.sv_cmd: STILL OPEN: No sv_cmd anticheat admin command. Base sv_cmd.qc:248 lets an admin dump anticheat_report_to_eventlog for a chosen client on demand. The port's server command table has no anticheat verb, so an admin cannot pull a live per-client verdict at all.

[logic] sv-anticheat.report.register_playerstats: STILL OPEN: anticheat_register_to_playerstats is not ported. Base pre-registers each anticheat event id via PlayerStats_GameReport_AddEvent (playerstats.qc:339) so unregistered events are dropped. The port's PlayerStats.Init (PlayerStats.cs:105-116) registers alivetime/latency/wins/matches/joins/accuracy/handicap slots only; Event() auto-accepts any key, so the anticheat feed still works WITHOUT registration — but the registration step itself is absent, a structural divergence from Base's register-then-emit contract.

[logic/values] sv-antilag.solidmask.corpse: Base temporarily switches the shooter's hit-contents mask to also include CORPSE so a hitscan can hit gibbed/corpse bodies during the antilag trace (tracebox_antilag_force_wz, and W_SetupShot's pre-trace mask); the port's FireBullet/FireRailgunBullet/SetupShot do not adjust any contents mask around the trace. The port HAS corpse entities (Solid.Corpse / Entity.IsCorpse), so corpse-hitting behavior may differ unless the port's default trace already includes corpse-solids.

[logic/values] sv-antilag.mode.client_verified_and_cursor: g_antilag modes 1 (verified client-side) and 3 (prydon cursor hitscan) are unimplemented: a server set to either gets NO lag compensation at all (port treats anything != 2 as no-rewind).

[logic] sv-antilag.clear.on_spawn: History is wiped on a per-tick origin-jump heuristic (>150u) rather than an explicit spawn/teleport/vehicle-enter call; a (re)spawn or teleport that lands within TeleportTickDistance of the previous origin would not clear the ring, so a shot could briefly rewind the player toward a stale position.

[logic] sv-antilag.melee.secondary: Shotgun melee secondary is not lag-compensated: Base traces each swing-arc segment through WarpZone_traceline_antilag (shotgun.qc:124); the port's Shotgun.Melee (Shotgun.cs:238) uses a plain trace, so a high-ping melee can miss a moving target it would connect with in Base.

[logic] sv-campaign.preinit.gametype_switch: Campaign.Validate() (the Campaign_Invalid logic — gametype-mismatch + map-mismatch -> Bailout) exists and IS called live at the tail of PreInit and the entry of PostInit. BUT the two probes it reads (LoadedMapName / GametypeMismatch) are STILL never assigned by the host (Wave-5 wired IsCampaign/NextLevelHandler/CampaignLevelWarpHandler/SwitchGameTypeHandler in Boot but NOT these two). grep confirms ZERO assignments of Campaign.LoadedMapName / Campaign.GametypeMismatch in src/+game/. With both probes null, Validate() skips both checks and always returns true — so the guard is present but INERT:

[logic] sv-campaign.bots.may_start: Confirmed STILL wired ONLY into bot MOVEMENT (BotPopulation.MovementHeld checks g_campaign && !Campaign.BotsMayStart). The round-handler campaign gate (Base round_handler.qc:38) is EXPLICITLY DEFERRED in the port (RoundHandler.cs:41 comment mentions 'campaign bot ...'). ; Base also keys monster move (sv_monsters.qc:846) + monster think (1165), the campcheck mutator (sv_campcheck.qc:51), and the LMS campaign-start gate (sv_lms.qc:85) off campaign_bots_may_start; none of these subsystems consult Campaign.BotsMayStart in the port (grep finds the only consumer is BotPopulation.MovementHeld).

[values] sv-chat.flood.per_type_throttle: Line-wrap width is an integer 82 visible chars vs Base's 82.4289758859709 px-equiv (perl averagewidth on gfx/vera-sans.width); proportional per-char widths are not modeled, so long-message line-count / flood==2 'too long' boundary can differ by one char on edge-length messages.

[logic] sv-chat.routing.recipients: No active_minigame routing branch: a say_team from a player inside a ported minigame is routed as a normal team/spectator say (minigame players are usually observers), not to co-session participants; the :chat_minigame: event-log line is never emitted (minigames exist in the port: MinigameSessionManager). Player has no ActiveMinigame link. ; INGAME(it) round-mode semantics not modeled: the port has no per-player .ingame round flag, so IsIngame(p) == !IsObserver(p). In round/elimination modes (Clan Arena / LMS / Survival) an eliminated-but-still-INGAME player is treated as a spectator, so the s

[logic/values] sv-chat.ignore.crud: IGNORE_MAXPLAYERS = 50 in the port (Chat.cs:427) vs Base 16 (cmd.qh:11, confirmed): a player can ignore up to 50 others before the 'list full' rejection instead of 16. The Chat.cs:427 doc-comment mislabels 50 as 'QC IGNORE_MAXPLAYERS', so this is an unintended value drift, not a flagged divergence. ; Permanent (db-persisted) ignore tier is dropped (the QC return-2 path / ServerProgsDB by UID); ignores are keyed by PersistentId in-memory only (acceptable within a match, lost across server restarts).

[values] sv-chat.format.macros: PlayerHealth 'observing' is recognized only for health == -2342; Base also treats (health == 2342 && mapvote_initialized) as 'observing' (the mapvote screen). Confirmed at chat.qc:466. Minor edge case.

[logic/values] sv-chat.format.crosshair_tokens: %y (aim location) and %x (aimed entity) have no server-side crosshair trace (WarpZone_crosshair_trace_plusvisibletriggers) wired into chat: %y falls back to the player's own location (NearestLocation(self.Origin)) and %x always returns 'nothing' (Chat.cs:553/560). ; %d uses self-origin (NearestLocation(self.Origin)) instead of the player's death_origin (chat.qc:564), so '%d' names the live location, not where they last died. ; NearestLocation has no item fallback: Base falls back to the nearest pickup item's .netname via findnearest(checkitems=true) when no target_location matches (chat.qc:453

[logic] sv-chat.delivery.dedicated_print_gating: Base dedicated_print (main.qc:233-236) prints ONLY when autocvar_sv_dedicated; the port's DedicatedPrint (Chat.cs:666) unconditionally invokes ChatConsole, wired to GD.Print (NetGame.cs:745/776). On a listen server (sv_dedicated 0) this surfaces every routed public/team/spectator chat line — and every tell when g_chat_tellprivacy 0 — on the host's stdout where Base prints nothing. Cosmetic / host-console-only; no client-visible effect.

[logic] sv-cheats.gating.cheats_allowed: Missing the sv_clones clone-allowance branch (CHIMPULSE CLONE_* permitted while this.lip < sv_clones regardless of sv_cheats) — moot until the CLONE impulses exist, but the branch is absent. Unchanged by Wave-5.
[logic/values/timing] sv-cheats.impulse.clone: No CopyBody / corpse-clone mechanic; the clone impulses do nothing and sv_clones is unread. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/values/timing] sv-cheats.impulse.speedrun: No personal_wp snapshot/restore (origin/velocity/resources/weapons/items/statuseffects/pause-timers), no AbortSpeedrun hook, no speedrunning flag handling. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/values/timing] sv-cheats.impulse.teleport: No emergency player-teleport cheat: neither the info_autoscreenshot path nor a player-side MoveToRandomMapLocation exists. (Note: a BallEntity.Relocate / MoveToRandomMapLocation exists for the Keepaway/Nexball/Invasion BALL, but that is the gametype ball-respawn mechanic, NOT the player-teleport cheat — different consumer, not reusable here.) Unchanged by Wave-5.
[logic/values] sv-cheats.impulse.r00t: No r00t cheat: missing the random-enemy RadiusDamage(1000), the EFFECT_ROCKET_EXPLODE visual, and the SND_ROCKET_IMPACT sound. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic] sv-cheats.cmd.teleporttotarget: No teleporttotarget command: Base spawns cheattriggerteleport + teleport_findtarget + Simple_TeleportPlayer; not implemented (no case, no Register, no allowlist entry). Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/values] sv-cheats.cmd.particles_make: No pointparticles/trailparticles (effect emission along the crosshair/aim line), no make (spawn a func_breakable model at the aim point), no penalty (race penalty time). None have a Cheats.Command case. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/values/timing] sv-cheats.frame.drag: No CheatFrame per-frame hook and no Drag subsystem: cannot pick up/carry/throw entities (cheat or normal-range), no te_lightning1 drag beam. Unchanged by Wave-5. ; No dragbox_spawn/dragpoint_spawn/drag_remove/drag_setcnt/drag_save/drag_saveraceent/drag_clear race-checkpoint map-editor commands. ; g_grab_range cvar exists (Cvars.cs:353) but is never read (no consumer).

[logic/values] sv-cheats.mapent.info_autoscreenshot: No info_autoscreenshot spawnfunc: the entity is dropped on map load and the TELEPORT cheat's preferred destination never exists. ; g_max_info_autoscreenshot cvar exists but with default 60, not Base's 3 (xonotic-server.cfg:643); moot since nothing reads it, but the default is wrong. STILL not corrected by Wave-5 (re-verified Base=3, port=60 on 2026-06-25).

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.connect.client_connect: Connect-side server->client channel deferred (see connect.fix_client_cvars_welcome row): no clientstuff/FixClientCvars/clientversion/_teams_available stuffcmds, no sv_notice_join/MOTD, no Handicap_Initialize/playban/chatban, no model_randomizer, no Send_NextMap. ; Player_DetermineForcedTeam / forced-team-otherwise spectator routing not modeled (team is pre-assigned via AssignBestTeam only).

[logic/values/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.connect.fix_client_cvars_welcome: No server->client stuffcmd/welcome channel: FixClientCvars (cl_jumpspeedcap_min/max, cl_shootfromfixedorigin, prediction/gentle), clientstuff, cmd clientversion, set _teams_available, sv_notice_join/SendWelcomeMessage (MOTD/hostname/map/mutators), Send_NextMap_To_Player, and the ClientInit_misc per-client init entity (armor-blockpercent, damagepush speedfactor, fog, serverflags, g_trueaim_minrange, hook/arc shot origins) are all unmodeled. ; The port instead pushes a few physics-relevant cvars via per-client replicated-cvar tables; there is no MOTD, no version request, and no per-client init-e

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.join.join_player: Join queue + team-selection ordering not modeled: the queued_join TeamBalance_AreEqual two-pass (queued-team dibs first pass + strongest-autoselect-by-skill second pass, player_with_dibs conflict) is absent; the port autoselects via a single connect-time AssignBestTeam. ; Campaign Join branch (campaign_bots_may_start / ReadyRestart) only partially modeled (CampaignHostAutojoin opts the host in); the bot-start ReadyRestart trigger is not in ClientManager.Join. ; Queue-conflict + queued-player branches still deferred: CENTER_JOIN_PLAY_TEAM_QUEUECONFLICT (depends on the unmodeled wants_join/playe

[logic/values] sv-client-lifecycle.join.join_allowed: Only MIN_SPEC_TIME + (teamplay && lockteams) enforced. Missing: version_mismatch, Player_GetForcedTeamIndex==TEAM_FORCE_SPECTATOR, g_playban_list, g_maxping (ping<=0 too-soon + ping>maxping), and the whole QueueNeeded/queuePlayer/ShowTeamSelection + nJoinAllowed/GetPlayerLimit/g_maxplayers/bots_would_leave capacity gate. ; No CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT / CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT_VERSIONMISMATCH / CENTER_JOIN_PLAYBAN / CENTER_JOIN_PREVENT_PING / CENTER_TEAMCHANGE_LOCKED centerprints for blocked joins.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.spawn.put_player_in_server: In-PutPlayerInServer team assignment (bot_forced_team / wants_join>0 SetPlayerTeam / TeamBalance_JoinBestTeam) is not run here — the port pre-assigns the team once at connect, so a mid-match team change or wants_join preference isn't honored on respawn. ; SUB_UseTargets(spot) (spawnpoint .target firing on spawn, with the assault/race target-suppression hack) is NOT modeled in this path (TargetUtilities owns SUB_UseTargets but nothing calls it for the spawn spot). ; wr_resetplayer + reload-all-reloadable-weapons-on-spawn (weapon_load = reloading_ammo) and CL_SpawnWeaponentity per slot are flagg

[logic/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.spawn.spawn_shield: The (!sv_ready_restart_after_countdown && time < game_starttime) countdown extension (roll shield + pauserotarmor/health + pauseregen forward by game_starttime - time) is NOT applied: a player who spawns during the pre-match countdown loses spawn protection and starts rotting earlier than Base. Note Base does NOT extend pauserotfuel_finished in this block; only shield + armor-rot + health-rot + regen.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.observer.put_observer_in_server: Still omits: SelectObservePoint / observe-spawn placement (the port keeps the current origin instead), VoteCount/ReadyCount recount, the SPECTATE_WARNING / sv_maxplayers_spectator_blocktime block, CHAT_NOSPECTATORS notice, RemoveGrapplingHooks/Portal_ClearAll, alivetime PlayerStats report, and the is_forced/use_spawnpoint signature (the port has no is_forced/use_spawnpoint params).

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.observer.observer_think: SpectatePrev (impulse 12/16/19/220-229), the IMP_weapon_drop CAMERA_SPECTATOR cycle, MinigameImpulse handling, the +use clippedspectating MOVETYPE_FLY toggle, and the sv_spectate 2 'spectating not allowed' block are not modeled.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.observer.autojoin_delayed: g_maxping / forced-team (TEAM_FORCE_SPECTATOR) / AutoJoinOnConnection mutator hook / (!teamplay || g_balance_teams) gates deferred. The QC `ping > 0` precondition (delay until centreprints work) is not modeled. sv_spectate and g_campaign holds ARE honored.

[logic/values/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.dead.respawn_countdown_announce: ShowRespawnCountdown announcer (10-9-8 CNT_RESPAWN) never fires: RespawnTiming sets Player.RespawnCountdown but DeadPlayerThink never calls a ShowRespawnCountdown equivalent to decrement it or play Announcer_PickNumber(CNT_RESPAWN, n). The 0.5s overlap-prevention guard is also unported.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.player_powerups: MF_ROCKET jetpack modelflag (IT_USING_JETPACK), EF_NODEPTHTEST/EF_FULLBRIGHT (g_nodepthtestplayers/g_fullbrightplayers), the EF_NODEPTHTEST clear-each-frame, and player_powerups_remove_all-on-death are not modeled. ; The SUPERWEAPON pickup/lost/broken centerprints (CENTER_SUPERWEAPON_PICKUP/LOST/BROKEN) + INFO_SUPERWEAPON_PICKUP notification + play_countdown(SND_POWEROFF) are absent.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.pressed_keys: The server never sets the networked PRESSED_KEYS stat (no GetPressedKeys-equivalent in the per-frame path). The local-player HUD pressedkeys/strafe panel works because PressedKeysPanel computes the bitmask CLIENT-SIDE from local bind state, but a SPECTATED player's held keys (PressedKeysPanel.PressedKeysOverride) are never fed by the net layer, and the QC observer-clears-PRESSED_KEYS rule (PlayerPostThink) is absent. ; GetPressedKeys mutator hook + the IS_DUCKED crouch-key workaround are not modeled server-side.

[logic/values/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.idle_kick: Idle players are never kicked or demoted to spectator; the idle countdown bangs (SND TALK2 at countdown_time), CENTER_DISCONNECT_IDLING / CENTER_MOVETOSPEC_IDLING centerprints, and INFO_QUIT_KICK_IDLING / INFO_MOVETOSPEC_IDLING notifications never appear. ; The alivetime parm_idlesince afk-gate (pause alivetime accrual after 30s idle) is not modeled, so xonstat skill-per-second would be skewed by afk time.

[logic/values/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.spectator_kick: When sv_spectate is disabled, QC warns then drops a client that stays a spectator/observer past g_maxplayers_spectator_blocktime (SPECTATE_WARNING MSG_MULTI then INFO_QUIT_KICK_SPECTATING + dropclient_schedule). The port reads sv_spectate only to gate the autojoin hold; it never warns or kicks a stuck spectator.

[logic/values/timing] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.name_and_version: Outdated/old-client/beta version-nag INFO notifications (INFO_VERSION_OUTDATED/OLD/BETA via vercmp + version_nagtime) never sent. ; Names are not truncated to sv_name_maxlength, invisible names not replaced with Player#<id>, the :name: event-log line and 'name was truncated/invisible' sprints are absent. ; GOD MODE info (INFO_GODMODE_OFF when FL_GODMODE drops) not modeled.

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.frame.prethink_misc: UpdateChatBubble (the chatbubble.spr / minigame_busy bubble over a typing or minigame-busy player, ChatBubbleThink + ShowChatBubble hook) is not modeled — no visible 'is typing' bubble. ; The PlayerPreThink +use edge dispatch (PlayerUseKey) is not a single ported function: individual +use consumers (buttons/vehicles/ctf) read the button directly, but the usekeypressed edge tracker + PlayerUseKey routing (and its spectate/minigame branches) are scattered/absent. ; SetZoomState (server-side weapon-zoom state for the zoom HUD/FOV net stat) and the PreThink teamkill_soundtime/taunt_soundtime voice

[logic] sv-client-lifecycle.disconnect.client_disconnect: ignore-list cleanup, Portal_ClearAll/RemoveGrapplingHooks, chatbubble/killindicator/personal/g_counters delete, vehicle exit, minigame part, WaypointSprite_PlayerGone, ReadyCount/VoteCount-on-leave, player_powerups_remove_all (poweroff sound), sv_hook_lastleave, and TeamBalance_RemoveExcessPlayers / QueuedPlayersTagIn are not all modeled.

[logic/values/timing] sv-clientkill.antispam.penalty: No escalating delay on repeated `kill`; the next-allowed-kill carry-forward (clientkill_nexttime; killtime=max(killtime, nexttime-time)) does not exist. KillCountdown.Begin re-uses the existing cnt on a repeat `kill` but never raises the floor by the antispam window.

[logic/values/timing] sv-clientkill.indicator.entity: No floating numeric countdown model is attached above the player; nothing is networked for it (KillCountdown drives only the announcer + center-print, explicitly NOT the digit entity — KillCountdown.cs:13). ; Clones on g_clones ghosts (the IL_EACH(g_clones) killindicator copy on each non-respawnghost clone) are also absent. ; The digit-entity colormod tint (black / grey / Team_ColorRGB) is unmodeled.

[logic/timing] sv-clientkill.teamchange.deferred: Team change / spectate still happen instantly (CmdSelectTeam sets Team + KillPlayerForTeamChange Teamplay.cs:442 → immediate Combat.Damage; CmdSpectate calls PutObserverInServer immediately). They do NOT route through KillCountdown.Begin, so no countdown / cancel window / TEAMCHANGE_* print fires for a team move. ; Missing the auto-select (-1) special cases: defer-to-Join when team<=0 (team_selected=-1 + return), and the 'already best team' early-out (CENTER_TEAMCHANGE_ALREADYBEST). The port's CmdSelectTeam auto path resolves AssignBestTeam and applies it immediately, with a plain 'already on

[logic] sv-clientkill.mutator.hooks: No ClientKill mutator hook anywhere in src/ (re-grepped: zero EV_ClientKill / OnClientKill / CanClientKill symbols). A frozen freezetag/CA player CAN self-kill: FreezeTag.cs:267 sets IsFrozen + :582 MaxHealth=1f on freeze (NOT IsDead), so CmdKill's only gates (FragsSpectator at Commands.cs:1317 / IsDead at :1318) miss a frozen player, and KillCountdown's eventual PerformKill (Combat.Damage 100000) kills them — defeating the freeze. Base ft hook returns STAT(FROZEN) to forbid it. (Now lands after the 2s delay rather than instantly, but still lands.) ; cts/race killtime=0 overrides are not model

[logic/values] sv-clientkill.silent.cts_finish: On delay expiry the port respawns the runner (OnFinishRetract = Clients.Spawn, GameWorld.cs:1906) instead of running a DEATH_KILL/ClientKill_Silent, so the finish self-kill yields no death event / obituary / death stat — only a teleport-to-start. It does not use the kill machinery at all. ; The port does not handle the -1 = instant or 0 = never cases: ScheduleRetract (Cts.cs:354) uses MathF.Max(0f, delay), so g_cts_finish_kill_delay -1 schedules now+0 (≈instant) AND 0 also schedules now+0 (fires immediately) — but Base 0 means DO NOT KILL. Both -1 and 0 collapse to 'retract this frame'. Base s

[logic] sv-clientkill.impulse.waypoint_kill: Base routes the personal-waypoint clear impulse (waypoint_clear*) through ClientKill when (g_cts||g_race) && g_allow_checkpoints, letting a speedrunner reset to their personal checkpoint. The port's CmdWaypointClear (Commands.cs:1393) only removes the sprite (ClearPersonal/ClearOwned) and explicitly does NOT model the personal-checkpoint teleport-reset kill, so this kill caller is absent.

[logic] sv-commands-votes.vote.call: Wave-2 ADDED the QC gate chain in the correct order (sv_vote_gamestart/game_starttime, IS_CLIENT, timeout_status && cmd!='timein') but the predicates are UNWIRED in GameWorld: VoteController.TimeoutActive defaults () => false, GameStartTime defaults () => 0f (so Now < 0 is never true), IsClient defaults _ => true. Net effect: the timeout-active guard and the pre-match game_starttime guard never fire — the new code is present but DEAD on the live path. ; MISSING the !IS_CLIENT(caller) guard in effect (IsClient unwired, default true) — low impact, the caller is generally already connected. ; ANN

[logic] sv-commands-votes.vote.parse_whitelist: map/nextmap parse still does not run ValidateMap/MapInfo_FixName nor the sv_vote_override_mostrecent recent-map block: an invalid or recently-played map name is accepted into the vote. ; No per-command arg restriction (VoteCommand_checkargs / sv_vote_command_restriction_<cmd> charlist + min-arg count) is applied.

[logic] sv-commands-votes.vote.count: Wave-2 changed the real-player predicate to IsPlayer(it) || InGame(it) (correct QC IS_PLAYER||INGAME shape) but BOTH predicates are unwired in GameWorld: IsPlayer defaults _ => true and InGame defaults _ => false. realPlayers therefore still == all clients, so the spectator-exclusion branch (!spectators_allowed && realPlayers>0) STILL never drops spectator ballots under sv_vote_nospectators 2.

[logic] sv-commands-votes.vote.master: sv_vote_master_ids (auto-login by crypto fingerprint on connect) is still not honored. ; Wave-2 ADDED the timeout_status guard to the master-vote-call branch (CmdMaster), but VoteController.TimeoutActive is unwired (defaults () => false) so the guard is DEAD — same root cause as vote.call.

[values/timing] sv-commands-votes.timeout.state_machine: Wave-2 ADDED slowmo machinery (SlowmoValue 0.0001 const + ApplySlowmo sink + OrigSlowmo capture/restore) to TimeoutController.Think, but ApplySlowmo is NOT wired in GameWorld and the world's GameStopped still includes Timeout.IsPaused (a hard freeze). So the engine slowmo cvar is never set and players still cannot inch during the pause — the slowmo gap is unchanged (code present but dead). ; Wave-2 ADDED per-second CENTER_TIMEOUT_BEGINNING/ENDING center-print + ANNCE_PREPARE machinery (EmitCountdown firing CenterPrintBeginning/CenterPrintEnding/AnnouncePrepare), but those sinks are NOT wired i

[logic/values] sv-commands-votes.common.info_commands: who still omits the ping / packetloss / jointime / crypto_id columns and the sv_status_privacy hiding (only prints index/name/team/bot/address; address shown only on server console).
[values] sv-commands-votes.admin.teamplay_match: shuffleteams still uses a deterministic round-robin over a stable order rather than QC FOREACH_CLIENT_RANDOM (not randomized).

[logic/values] sv-commands-votes.debug_tools.missing: radarmap is not registered: no in-game radar-image (XPM) generation for the minimap. ; gettaginfo / animbench / bbox / trace / stuffto / adminmsg / anticheat / delrec / database / effectindexdump / make_mapinfo / printstats console verbs are unimplemented (no Register() calls).

[logic] sv-handicap.init.defaults_to_one: No explicit per-connect/per-spawn Handicap_Initialize; forced fields rely on the 1f construction default. Equivalent at the default but there is no reset call + no handicap_level update on init. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/values] sv-handicap.level.compute_and_network: handicap_level (0-16) is never computed or networked, and the scoreboard player_handicap icon is absent: handicapped players are visually indistinguishable. Unchanged by Wave-5 (a generic map_bound_ranges exists for DamageText/Dodging but is not applied to handicap level).

[logic/values] sv-handicap.scoreboard.handicap_icon: Scoreboard player_handicap icon is absent: handicapped players are visually indistinguishable. Depends on the (still-missing) handicap_level. Unchanged by Wave-5.

[logic/timing] sv-handicap.dynamic.mutator: Recompute cadence differs (PlayerSpawn/PlayerDies vs Base's ClientDisconnect/PutClientInServer/MakePlayerObserver/AddedPlayerScore(SP_SCORE)); roster shrink (leave/observe) and non-death score deltas (caps/objective points) leave the mean stale until the next spawn/death. ; Application iterates players-with-Client-flag, not Base's FOREACH_CLIENT(true) which also clamps spectators' forced handicap. ; Sets HandicapGive/Take directly rather than via Handicap_SetForcedHandicap, so it never triggers Handicap_UpdateHandicapLevel (moot until handicap_level exists).

[logic] sv-impulse.dispatch.commands_gate: minigame impulses STILL not routed: QC runs MinigameImpulse(this, imp) FIRST (impulse.qc:379, even before the timeout gate); the port has MinigameSessionManager.Impulse but DispatchImpulse never calls it (verified: no Minigames.Impulse caller in Commands.cs/ServerNet.cs). A player inside a minigame pressing a weapon key drives weapon selection instead of a minigame move. ; minor ordering divergence: QC dispatches MinigameImpulse BEFORE the timeout gate; the port gates Intermission.Running + Timeout.IsPaused first. Inconsequential while minigame routing is absent, but means even once minigames

[logic/values] sv-impulse.weapon.priority_cycle: STILL OPEN: all ten priority impulses cycle the SINGLE main cl_weaponpriority list. QC weapon_priority_handle (impulse.qc:100) cycles cvar_cl_weaponpriorities[number] = cl_weaponpriority0..9. PriorityHandle (WeaponImpulses.cs:136) still takes only `dir`, never the group `number`, and WeaponPriority/PriorityProvider only ever return cl_weaponpriority (the N-suffixed lists are replicated/fixed-up but never read by the impulse path). Wave-2 added an Inventory.cs:183 comment referencing the per-group lists but did NOT wire them. Observable: a priority-group bind selects from the full weapon list,

[logic] sv-impulse.weapon.nextprev_singletons: weapon_next/prev_bygroup (18/19) cycle the priority list, not weaponorder_byimpulse: Inventory.NextWeapon/PreviousWeapon list==1 falls back to WeaponPriority (commented 'by-impulse order not modeled'). Observable: 'next/prev by group' wheel cycling order differs from Base. Unchanged by Wave-2.

[logic] sv-impulse.weapon.getcycle: The impulse-group complain path is simplified: QC computes have_other and a custom-group bitmask for imp<0 (selection.qc:148-171); the port omits both since it drives the imp=-1 cycle/best path. The weaponsInMap (map-spawned-but-unowned weapon) branch (selection.qc:166,187) is unmodeled, so unowned-but-on-map weapons are skipped (cannot be cycle-selected before pickup). Unchanged by Wave-2.

[logic] sv-impulse.use.usekey: STILL OPEN: the PlayerUseKey MUTATOR-hook half is never fired. VehicleBoarding.UseKey (the port's PlayerUseKey entry) handles ONLY vehicle enter/exit — it has no trailing PlayerUseKey hook dispatch. So CTF flag throw/pass/request-pass, Keepaway/Team Keepaway/Nexball/KeyHunt voluntary ball/key drop, and door/objective/button 'use' are all unreachable. Confirmed: Ctf.HandleUseKey (Ctf.cs:1333) exists but has ZERO callers; grep finds no PlayerUseKey mutator-hook dispatch anywhere.

[logic/values] sv-impulse.waypoints.sprites: STILL MISSING: none of the waypoint impulses are implemented or routed: personal/here/danger at location/crosshair-trace/death-origin, the HELP-ME follow ping (HelpMePing hook), and clear-personal / clear-all (which also ClientKill in race/cts checkpoint modes when g_allow_checkpoints). A player cannot deploy or clear personal/team waypoint sprites via impulse. Unchanged by Wave-2.

[logic/values] sv-impulse.cheat.impulses: STILL OPEN: cheat impulses 140-148 (clone/speedrun/teleport/r00t/drag) are not dispatched — they need engine services and remain unported (Cheats.cs doc-comment defers them). Only GIVE_ALL (99) is routed.

[logic] sv-intermission.matchend.nextlevel: NextLevel reproduces the winner latch (MarkWinners), the :gameover event log (sv_eventlog gated), PlayerStats_GameReport, the campaign PreIntermission decision, and the demo stop — but OMITS VoteReset(true), Kill_Notification(NOTIF_ALL … kill all centerprints), WeaponStats_Shutdown, DumpStats(true), the MatchEnd_BeforeScores / MatchEnd mutator hooks, and the localcmd(sv_hook_gameend) trailing hook.

[logic/values] sv-intermission.nextmap.override: quit_when_empty is not implemented: a server that would start an empty next map does not quit. ; quit_and_redirect is not implemented: players are not redirected/reconnected to another server at match end. ; lastlevel (single-player 'show menu after match') is not implemented (the cvar is registered but never read in DriveEndOfMatchMapFlow).

[logic] sv-intermission.matchend.nextmap_broadcast: Base stuffcmds 'settemp _nextmap <map>' to every real client whenever the queued next map changes, so the client knows the upcoming map; the port never broadcasts it. ScoreboardPanel.NextMap (drawn as the 'Next map: …' line at ScoreboardPanel.cs:740-742, QC GET_NEXTMAP) exists but is never assigned anywhere, so the scoreboard always shows a blank next-map line.

[logic/values] sv-intermission.client.view_freeze: No SVC_INTERMISSION equivalent: the local view is not locked to an intermission camera, the viewmodel is not hidden (EF_NODRAW), and the -2342 health sentinel is not set. ; MatchIntermission is networked but NetGame uses it only to gate the HUD low-health shake (_fullHud.Intermission) and the TIMER panel's intermission time — it does not freeze the view.

[logic] sv-intermission.client.scoreboard_autoshow: The scoreboard is shown only while the +showscores key is held (NetGame.UpdateScoreboard reads BindTable.ShowScores); Base force-draws it at intermission==1 (and hides it at intermission==2 when the mapvote owns the screen). No intermission branch in UpdateScoreboard.

[logic/values/timing] sv-intermission.client.autoscreenshot: No autoscreenshot stuffcmd dance at intermission (the autoscreenshot timer + cl_autoscreenshot_screenshot stuffcmd in IntermissionThink): the server cannot force clients to capture the end-of-match scoreboard. ; cl_autoscreenshot is now ACCEPTED by the server (Commands.SentCvarAllowlist includes it, Commands.cs:199 — the prior audit's 'server drops it' is fixed), so the client's opt-in reaches the per-client store. BUT there is still no server-side consumer, no sv_autoscreenshot cvar default, no intermission-time autoscreenshot timer, and no client screenshot trigger. The replication is stored

[logic/values] sv-intermission.audio.cdtrack: No sv_intermission_cdtrack handling: the random intermission CD track (RandomSelection over the cdtrack words → 'cd loop <track>' stuffcmd) is never selected or looped. ; target_music_kill() at NextLevel (stop map music at match end) is NOT reproduced: MusicPlayer.cs has no intermission/match-state awareness, so map music keeps playing through the scoreboard.

[logic] sv-ipban.enforce.on_connect: Base uses Ban_MaybeEnforceBanOnce (a one-shot .ban_checked flag) AND re-checks on the client's first command (cmd.qc:1181); the port enforces only once on connect (GameWorld.cs:768) with no first-command re-check. Low impact (connect-time drop is dominant). UNCHANGED by Wave-2.

[logic] sv-ipban.insert: The dosync leg (OnlineBanList_SendBan, with the reason!="" and not-~-prefixed guard) is dropped — no online ban propagation on insert (see sv-ipban.sync.online). UNCHANGED by Wave-2.

[logic/timing] sv-ipban.persist: No shutdown Ban_SaveBans (Base saves at world.qc:2631). The port saves on every Insert/Delete so the list stays current, but a long session's persisted seconds-remaining is not refreshed at session end. UNCHANGED by Wave-2. ; Load does not spawn the bansyncer think entity (Base Ban_LoadBans creates one); moot here because online sync is absent. UNCHANGED by Wave-2.

[logic] sv-ipban.softban.chatban: Connect-time mute re-application is STILL missing: Base client.qc:1246 re-mutes any connecting client found in g_chatban_list. Wave-2 added a Bans.IsChatBanned(p) seam helper (Bans.cs:459) BUT IT HAS NO LIVE CALLER — InfraClientConnect (GameWorld.cs:762) never invokes it, so a muted player who reconnects (or persists across a map change) can talk again. The helper is dead code (only its def exists; grep finds no source caller).

[values] sv-ipban.softban.playban: STILL OPEN (values): g_playban_minigames default is 1 in the port (Cvars.cs:347) but 0 in Base (xonotic-server.cfg:436). Wave-2 did NOT change this default — it only wired the active-removal call behind the (mis-defaulted) gate, so out of the box the port still wrongly removes/blocks play-banned players from minigames. ; STILL OPEN (liveness): connect-time playban re-application is missing — Base client.qc:1243 TRANSMUTEs connecting clients in g_playban_list to Observer. Wave-2 added Bans.IsPlayBanned(p) (Bans.cs:467) BUT IT HAS NO LIVE CALLER; InfraClientConnect never re-spectates, so a play-

[logic] sv-mapvoting.ballot.build: Suggestion seeding is LIVE: GameWorld.cs:2431-2434 maps Commands.MapSuggestions into (Map, Suggester) pairs and passes them to the 4-arg Start, which seeds min(g_maplist_votable_suggestions, nmax) suggested maps first, deduped, then fills from the rotation (matching MapVote_AddVotableMaps' smax seed). ; BUT the suggester NAME is always lost: Commands.MapSuggestions is IReadOnlyList<string> (map names only — CmdSuggestMap never records who suggested), and GameWorld passes Suggester: "" for every entry. So show_suggester has nothing to surface even though the plumbing exists (NetGame forwards c.

[logic/values/timing] sv-mapvoting.vote_scoreboard_hide: Base MapVote_Tick forces RES_HEALTH=2342 on every client (incl. bots) and sends SVC_FINALE "" once, to hide the scoreboard during the vote; the port models none of this (the only -2342 in src/ is an unrelated 'observing' marker in Chat.cs:579). ; Base zeroes CS(it).impulse each tick after reading it; the port reads the impulse synchronously in DispatchImpulse (no stored per-tick impulse field to zero), so this specific clear is moot — but the scoreboard-hide and sentinel health are genuinely absent.

[logic/values/timing] sv-mapvoting.gametype_vote: No server-side gametype vote at all: no gametype ballot, no custom gametypes, no hook execution, no MapInfo_SwitchGameType, no maplist reset (grep: no GameTypeVote_* counterpart in src/). ; sv_vote_gametype_options still ships 'dm tdm ctf' (Cvars.cs:421) vs Base 'dm tdm ca ctf' (missing ca). ; sv_vote_gametype_reduce_time/_reduce_count/_detail/_default_current/_maplist_reset cvars are all still unregistered.

[logic] sv-mapvoting.suggestions: Suggested maps reach the ballot: GameWorld.cs:2431-2434 passes Commands.MapSuggestions into MapVote.Start, which seeds them ahead of the rotation (capped by g_maplist_votable_suggestions). So suggestmap genuinely affects the ballot on the live path. ; BUT the suggester NAME is dropped: CmdSuggestMap (Commands.cs:2058-2092) stores only the map string into _mapSuggestions (Commands.MapSuggestions is IReadOnlyList<string>), and GameWorld passes Suggester: "". So g_maplist_votable_show_suggester (registered, default 1) and the panel's 'Suggested by:' line can never populate even though NetGame for

[logic] sv-mapvoting.client.input: No input handler: arrows/enter/space/digits/mouse don't move a selection or cast a vote on the panel. The panel exposes a Selection property but NOTHING writes it from input (grep: no _Input/_GuiInput/InputEvent in MapVotePanel.cs; nothing in game/ assigns MapVote.Selection from input). ; No localcmd('impulse N') equivalent to send the chosen option to the server. The server-side CastVote IS reachable via the `impulse` command (Commands.cs:1585), so the only missing client piece is the input->impulse bridge. On a listen server the player can now SEE the ballot (Wave 5) but still must type `imp

[logic] sv-spawnpoints.select.select_spawn_point: Emergency fallback differs: Base, when Spawn_FilterOutBadSpots returns empty, RE-FILTERS the spot list once with targetcheck=false (server/spawnpoints.qc:421-435) before giving up. The port skips that re-filter and instead seeds every spot at (prio 0, weight 0), so the assault/active gate is never relaxed and the player may be placed in solid via the prio-0 fallback. UNCHANGED. ; anypoint / g_spawn_useallspawns path: Base skips Spawn_FilterOutBadSpots entirely and does Spawn_WeightedPoint(1,1,1) over ALL spots (spawnpoints.qc:413-416). The port still runs ScoreSpot (startsolid veto + restricti

[values] sv-spawnpoints.select.weighted_point: spot.cnt is not multiplied into the weight: a mapper distributing several spawnpoints in a small area with cnt>1 (to weight that cluster more) gets uniform weighting in the port. Default cnt is 1 so stock maps are unaffected. UNCHANGED.

[logic/values] sv-spawnpoints.select.spawn_evalfunc: Spawn_Score's race_spawns gate (a race spot MUST have a target, else -1) and the assault/race spawn_evalfunc target chain (findchain(targetname, spot.target) → targ.spawn_evalfunc rewrites/vetoes the score; no target found and not CTS ⇒ -1) are not ported (inline NOTE deferring this at SpawnSystem.cs:422). No port symbol implements the in-scoring biasing/veto, so race-checkpoint score-biasing and assault spawn redirection do not bias/veto spots. STILL OPEN. ; NOTE: Race's respawn-at-last-checkpoint IS now wired (Race.ApplyRespawnSpot via ClientManager.OnPreSpawn sets Player.SpawnPointTarg to t

[timing] sv-spawnpoints.put.put_player_in_server: The spawned player model gets no EF_TELEPORT_BIT | EF_RESTARTANIM_BIT: Base sets the teleport sparkle + anim-restart effect bits on respawn (client.qc:630); the port relies on an origin particle burst instead. The bit constants exist (CsqcModelEffectFlags.cs:43-44) but are never set on the player. STILL OPEN. ; The time < game_starttime countdown extension (roll the shield + rot/regen pauses forward by game_starttime - time) is not applied here (flagged inline; also tracked in sv-client-lifecycle). STILL OPEN.

[values] sv-spawnpoints.fx.spawn_event_particle: Spawn sound SND_SPAWN (cl_spawn_event_sound, bit1 of g_spawn_alloweffects 3) is never played: the port emits the EFFECT_SPAWN particle burst on spawn but no audio cue (grep SND_SPAWN/spawn_event_sound in src+game = none). STILL OPEN. ; Team-coloring of the burst (Team_ColorRGB(teamnum) / entcs_GetColor in teamplay, client/spawnpoints.qc:58-60) is not networked through a SpawnEvent entity — the port emits an untinted EffectEmitter.Emit('SPAWN', origin). STILL OPEN. (Distinct from the idle-glow tint, which IS team-colored — see spawnpoint_idle_glow.)

[logic] sv-spawnpoints.mutator.spawn_near_teammate: Residual relocation-branch defers: tracebox_hits_trigger_hurt (hurt-volume reject), checkpvs (PVS visibility), and the g_nades nade-in-range reject are still skipped (a faithful superset that may keep a spot QC would reject); weaponLocked and PHYS_INPUT_BUTTON_CHAT gates are always false (no plumbed input/weapon-lock); closetodeath uses the player's current origin not .death_origin. The sky-surface (Q3SURFACEFLAG_SKY) and lava/slime/water pointcontents rejects ARE applied. STILL OPEN (the residual defers), but no change since baseline.

[logic] sv-teamplay.join.find_best_team: Tie-break differs: Base picks size -> team strength (skill*score) -> RANDOM among ties; port picks smallest count -> CompareTeams strength (skill*score bias) -> lowest fixed index (deterministic, no randomization). ; Gametype-level callers (CTF/CA/FreezeTag/Dom/KeyHunt/TDM/TeamMayhem via TeamBalance.JoinSmallestTeam) apply count only, with no score/skill tiebreak at all. ; Base uses m_num_players_net (humans, deducts leavable bots) vs raw count; port always uses raw count.

[logic/values] sv-teamplay.join.skill_weighting: No TrueSkill ratings for humans: SkillProvider returns the bot's real BotSkill (0..10) for bots but a flat 5 for every human, and variance is a flat 1. So skill weighting now biases bot-only ties but human-vs-human equal-size ties still reduce to a plain count. ; No unranked-client factor: g_balance_teams_skill_unranked_factor (0.666) is registered but read nowhere; the unranked-skill down-weighting is not applied. ; No score-ratio team-strength RAMP: Base TeamBalance_CompareTeamsInternal scales strength by team_score ratio * min(1,(t-start)/timelimit)^1.5 after warmup; the port applies a flat

[logic/values/timing] sv-teamplay.balance.autobalance_bots: Base autobalances bots unconditionally; port gates on g_balance_teams||g_balance_teams_prevent_imbalance (QC explicitly comments that gate OUT). ; Base triggers synchronously whenever a human changes team; port only runs on a 3s polling timer, so a manual human switch isn't immediately compensated. ; Uses frag score, not SP_SCORE, to pick the bot to move; ordering can differ when frags != composite score.

[logic/values] sv-teamplay.forced.determine_team: Non-campaign id/IP-list path is still dead: g_forced_team_red/blue/yellow/pink and g_forced_team_otherwise remain unregistered cvars, so PlayerInList always reads an empty list and 'otherwise' reads empty -> default. Only the g_campaign_forceteam branch has data to act on. ; No force-spectate routing: DetermineForcedTeam can produce TEAM_FORCE_SPECTATOR (otherwise=spectate), but AssignBestTeam only honors a real team (1..4); no join path moves an unlisted/spectate-forced player to spectators.

[logic/values] sv-teamplay.queue.join_queue: No join-queue: players join immediately regardless of balance; the deficit-filling tag-in (specific-team-first then any-team) is absent. ; wants_join (WantsJoin) exists on Player but is never consulted for queueing.

[logic/values/timing] sv-teamplay.remove.excess_players: On a player leave that unbalances 2-team play, Base moves the NEWEST joiner on the overfull team to spectators after a 10s countdown nag; the port does nothing. ; No Remove_Countdown timer, no CENTER_MOVETOSPEC_REMOVE / INFO_MOVETOSPEC_REMOVE notifications.

[logic] sv-teamplay.admin.lock_shuffle_move: shuffleteams uses a deterministic round-robin in roster order; Base uses FOREACH_CLIENT_RANDOM (randomized assignment). ; moveplayer/team don't enforce lockteams backup/restore semantics of MoveToTeam (which temporarily disables the lock); behavior overlaps but isn't the exact MoveToTeam path.

[logic/values] sv-teamplay.color.set_player_colors: No clientcolors / pants+shirt color model: the 'color' command's non-teamplay re-color and the teamplay team-from-color derivation are absent. Team is set directly as a color code.

[logic] sv-teamplay.global.team_entities: No unified Team_* global entity API: per-team score lives in Scores, alive/owned-item counts and winner queries are reimplemented ad hoc per gametype (CA/Dom/Onslaught) rather than via Team_GetWinnerAliveTeam / Team_GetWinnerTeam_WithOwnedItems.

[logic] sv-teamplay.change.team_change_hooks: Base Player_SetTeamIndex fires Player_ChangeTeam (can return true to BLOCK the change) before applying, and Player_ChangedTeam after; the port's AssignBestTeam/KillPlayerForTeamChange/shuffle set Entity.Team directly with no pre/post mutator dispatch. ; No way for a mutator to veto or react to a team change (e.g. balance overrides, per-mode team-change side effects); the early-out 'already on this team' guard also lives only inside Player_SetTeamIndex in Base.

[logic/values] sv-world-rules.boot.worldspawn: matchid is just the map name (GameLog.MatchId = MapName, GameWorld.cs:773), not the QC <counter>.<unixtime>.<rand6> id (weakens eventlog match correlation). ; No ServerProgsDB load, no support-entity spawn pass at boot (RandomSeed/PingPLReport/Cheat covered separately).

[logic/values] sv-world-rules.warmup.serverflags: readlevelcvars sets the SERVERFLAG_ALLOW_FULLBRIGHT / SERVERFLAG_FORBID_PICKUPTIMER bits in `serverflags` (networked to clients) from sv_allow_fullbright / sv_forbid_pickuptimer; the port has no `serverflags` global at all. The client cannot gate fullbright player rendering or hide the item-pickup timer accordingly.

[logic] sv-world-rules.warmup.ready_restart: sv_ready_restart_after_countdown branch still unimplemented (default now corrected to 0, matching Base): reset_map always runs immediately at countdown start, never deferred to a restart_timer think at game_starttime. Only referenced in a comment (WeaponFireDriver.cs:321). ; Per-player restart policy still not ported: no sv_spectate==2 demotion, no re-join of queued (wants_join) players, no per-player allowed_timeouts reset, no alivetime_start clear in ReadyRestart_force.

[logic] sv-world-rules.warmup.reset_map: Entity .reset/.reset2 pass is still thin: only projectile-class entities are deleted (ResetMapObjects, GameWorld.cs:2638-2650); per-entity reset callbacks (movers/items back to spawn, team_saved restore) are not driven through the FOREACH_ENTITY_FLOAT_ORDERED reset list, and the waypoint/assault .reset2 second pass is absent. ; No per-client accuracy_reset / Inventory_clear pass, and no shuffleteams_on_reset_map / reset_map_players mutator hooks beyond Mayhem/TeamMayhem total_damage_dealt.

[logic] sv-world-rules.checkrules.cascade: WinningCondition_RanOutOfSpawns (team spawn-exhaustion draw/win, g_spawn_useallspawns) is still not ported. ; leadlimit / leadlimit_and_fraglimit WIN semantics are still not evaluated in the world layer (the win comes purely from the gametype MatchEnded latch + tie report). NOTE: the leadlimit math now EXISTS in GameScores.CountFragsRemaining but only feeds the frags-remaining announcer, not the actual end-of-match decision. ; The CheckRules_World mutator hook (M_ARGV status override) is still not invoked.
[logic/values/timing] sv-world-rules.entity.randomseed: No shared server->client RNG seed: client-side random effects cannot be kept deterministic with the server.
[logic/values/timing] sv-world-rules.entity.pingplreport: No server->client ping / packet-loss / movement-loss report; scoreboard ping column and playerstats latency have no feed.

[logic/values/timing] sv-world-rules.boot.lightstyles: The 12 named animated lightstyles (flicker/pulse/candle/strobe, world.qc:882-920) plus style 63 are not installed at worldspawn: map lights driven by a style index will not animate.

[logic/values] sv-world-rules.misc.max_shot_distance: The global hitscan/aim clamp is a fixed constant 32768 in the port (WeaponFiring.MaxShotDistance, cs:39; TurretAI), NOT recomputed per-map from world bounds (min(230000, vlen(maxs-mins))). On maps whose diagonal exceeds 32768qu, hitscan/trueaim/turret traces stop short of where Base would reach; on tiny maps Base would clamp tighter than 32768.
[logic/values/timing] sv-world-rules.misc.redirection: No server-redirect / mass-reconnect command (sv_redirect_target / quit_and_redirect).

[logic] sv-world-rules.misc.shutdown: GameWorld.Shutdown is still a near-no-op (GameWorld.cs:984-987, only unsubscribes the cvar-changed handler). The QC Shutdown's slowmo-reset-on-active-timeout (cvar_set slowmo orig_slowmo when timeout_status==TIMEOUT_ACTIVE), the unfinished-match PlayerStats_GameReport(false), and bot_endgame are not performed on world teardown. ; Ban_SaveBans / ServerProgsDB persistence on shutdown: bans persist on add (Bans.Save at mutation) so the on-shutdown save is largely covered, but the persistent server DB (ServerProgsDB save/dump) has no port analogue here.
